message:
test(storage): add 12 tests for untested utility functions (br-n1kd1)

Cover previously untested storage functions:
- now_iso: RFC3339 format validation
- list_message_files: nonexistent/empty dir, recursive .md discovery
- resolve_attachment_source_path: empty input, relative-inside, traversal
  rejection, nonexistent file
- sanitize_browse_path: traversal variants, valid paths, backslash
  normalization
- check_archive_consistency: invalid timestamp → missing, missing_ids
  capped at 20

Storage crate: 147 → 159 tests (all passing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1366,6 +1366,7 @@
 {"id":"br-lzwth","title":"T6.3: Unit tests for log viewer adapter and severity mapping","description":"Test EventLogEntry adapter and LogViewer integration.

TEST CASES:

Severity mapping:
- ToolCallStart maps to DEBUG
- ToolCallEnd maps to DEBUG
- MessageSent maps to INFO
- MessageReceived maps to INFO
- AgentRegistered maps to INFO
- ReservationGranted maps to INFO
- ReservationReleased maps to INFO (force-release maps to WARN)
- HttpRequest maps to DEBUG
- HealthPulse maps to DEBUG
- ServerStarted maps to TRACE
- ServerShutdown maps to WARN
- All 11 event types have explicit mappings (no default/fallback needed)

Entry formatting:
- Timestamp formatted as HH:MM:SS.mmm
- Event type label is compact and consistent width
- Agent names included where available
- Message details truncated to fit line width

Filter presets:
- "All" filter passes all event types
- "Messages" filter passes only MessageSent + MessageReceived
- "Tools" filter passes only ToolCallStart + ToolCallEnd
- "Reservations" filter passes only ReservationGranted + ReservationReleased
- "Health" filter passes HealthPulse + ServerStarted + ServerShutdown
- Regex filter correctly matches on formatted entry text

Target: 15+ tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 11 severity mapping tests (one per event type)
- [ ] 3+ formatting tests
- [ ] 5+ filter preset and regex tests

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Implemented test hardening for log-viewer adapter semantics:\n- Added explicit 11-variant severity mapping test in tui_events.rs (severity_mapping_covers_all_event_variants_explicitly).\n- Added formatting-focused tests for timestamp zero-padding and multi-recipient summary compaction in tui_events.rs.\n- Added 5 preset coverage tests (All/Messages/Tools/Reservations/Health) in timeline.rs using seeded mixed event fixture.\n- Added regex filter behavior test for LogPane wrapper in console.rs (log_pane_regex_filter_matches_formatted_entries).\nValidation status: targeted rustfmt on touched files passed via rch; cargo test/check/clippy via rch blocked by remote workspace layout/throughput (repo-local rch missing sibling path crates; parent-dir sync jobs stalled and were cancelled).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonOx","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:59:54.596028994Z","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:17:58.714378314Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T17:17:58.714357085Z","close_reason":"Completed targeted log-viewer adapter + filtering test validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["log-viewer","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-lzwth","depends_on_id":"br-1rkm0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:00:28.864836913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-lzwth","depends_on_id":"br-30sl0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:00:30.705949170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":733,"issue_id":"br-lzwth","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validated `br-lzwth` test scope end-to-end on remote workers via `rch`:

- `rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path mcp_agent_mail_rust/Cargo.toml -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib severity_mapping_covers_all_event_variants_explicitly` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path mcp_agent_mail_rust/Cargo.toml -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib to_event_log_entry_` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path mcp_agent_mail_rust/Cargo.toml -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib filter_preset_` ✅ (5/5)
- `rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path mcp_agent_mail_rust/Cargo.toml -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib log_pane_regex_filter_matches_formatted_entries` ✅

Coverage in place for all requested categories:
- 11 explicit severity mappings (no fallback)
- formatting coverage for timestamp/entry formatting/recipient compaction
- preset filters (`All`, `Messages`, `Tools`, `Reservations`, `Health`)
- regex filtering against formatted log entries

No additional code edits were required in this pass; changes already present in:
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_events.rs`
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/timeline.rs`
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs`
","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:17:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-mkn3","title":"T6.1: Implement deterministic output capture with regex normalization (timestamps, PIDs, paths)","description":"## Objective
Implement deterministic golden-output capture normalization so noisy runtime-specific values do not create false diffs.

## Work
- Define normalization rules for timestamps, PIDs, paths, and other unstable tokens.
- Ensure normalization is reproducible and transparent to users diagnosing mismatches.
- Preserve semantically meaningful content while removing nondeterministic noise.

## Deliverable
A stable capture pipeline that makes golden comparisons trustworthy across environments.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:25:13.952576742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:00:34.076655786Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:00:34.076635759Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":223,"issue_id":"br-mkn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T6.1: Implement Deterministic Output Capture with Regex Normalization

## What to build
Run CLI commands, capture their output, and normalize non-deterministic content
(timestamps, PIDs, temp paths) so golden files are stable across runs. Replaces
the sed pipelines in bench_golden.sh.

## Current bash behavior
```bash
output=$(am --help 2>&1)
# Normalize timestamps like "2026-02-11T14:23:45Z"
output=$(echo "$output" | sed -E 's/[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9:]+Z/TIMESTAMP/g')
# Normalize PIDs
output=$(echo "$output" | sed -E 's/pid [0-9]+/pid PID/g')
# Normalize temp paths
output=$(echo "$output" | sed -E 's|/tmp/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+|/tmp/TMPDIR|g')
```

## Rust implementation
```rust
struct NormalizationRule {
    pattern: Regex,
    replacement: &'static str,
}

const DEFAULT_RULES: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
    // ISO-8601 timestamps
    (r"\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T[\d:]+(\.\d+)?Z?", "TIMESTAMP"),
    // Unix timestamps (microseconds)
    (r"\b1[67]\d{14}\b", "UNIX_MICROS"),
    // PIDs
    (r"pid \d+", "pid PID"),
    // Temp paths
    (r"/tmp/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+", "/tmp/TMPDIR"),
    // Cargo target dir
    (r"/data/tmp/cargo-target/[^\s]+", "CARGO_TARGET"),
    // Home directory
    (r"/home/\w+", "/home/USER"),
];

fn normalize_output(raw: &str, rules: &[NormalizationRule]) -> String

fn capture_command(cmd: &[String]) -> Result<CapturedOutput, Error> {
    let output = Command::new(&cmd[0]).args(&cmd[1..]).output()?;
    Ok(CapturedOutput {
        stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
        stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
        exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
    })
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Compile regexes once (lazy_static or OnceLock)
- Apply rules in order (some rules may interact)
- The normalization must be deterministic: same input → same output regardless of platform
- Consider normalizing ANSI color codes too (strip or preserve based on flag)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/golden.rs (new module)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:34:02Z"},{"id":256,"issue_id":"br-mkn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION from deep audit

Full golden capture list (27+ files):

CLI HELP TEXTS (17):
1. am --help
2. am --version
3. am inbox --help
4. am send --help
5. am search --help
6. am threads --help
7. am doctor --help
8. am agents --help
9. am lint --help
10. am typecheck --help
11. am share --help
12. am guard --help
13. am setup --help
14. am ci --help (NEW - after Track 1)
15. am flake-triage --help (NEW - after Track 2)
16. am bench --help (NEW - after Track 3)
17. am golden --help (self-referential, after Track 6 wiring)

MCP DENIAL MESSAGES (5):
18-22. Running CLI-only commands through mcp-agent-mail binary:
  mcp-agent-mail share → denial message
  mcp-agent-mail guard → denial message
  mcp-agent-mail lint → denial message
  mcp-agent-mail typecheck → denial message
  mcp-agent-mail setup → denial message

STUB ENCODER OUTPUTS (5):
23-27. Stub encoder with various inputs (deterministic outputs)

NORMALIZATION must strip:
- ANSI escape codes: \x1b\[[0-9;]*m and similar SGR sequences
- ISO-8601 timestamps: replace with <TIMESTAMP>
- PIDs: replace numeric PIDs with <PID>
- Absolute paths: replace /home/user/... with <HOME>/...
- Version strings: replace version numbers with <VERSION>

Each golden file: benches/golden/<name>.golden
Checksum file: benches/golden/checksums.sha256 (sha256sum format: "<hash>  <filename>")
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:51:03Z"},{"id":275,"issue_id":"br-mkn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T6.1 Normalization — Simpler Than Documented

The ACTUAL normalization in bench_golden.sh is THREE sed operations only:

1. ANSI escape codes: sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g'
   → Strips SGR sequences (colors/formatting)

2. ISO-8601 timestamps: sed 's/[0-9]\{4\}-[0-9]\{2\}-[0-9]\{2\}T[0-9:\.Z+-]*/TIMESTAMP/g'
   → Replaces timestamps with literal "TIMESTAMP"

3. Process IDs: sed 's/pid=[0-9]*/pid=PID/g'
   → Replaces "pid=12345" with "pid=PID"

IMPORTANT: Previous correction was WRONG about path and version normalization.
The bash does NOT normalize:
- Home directory paths (NO <HOME> replacement)
- Version strings (NO <VERSION> replacement)

Keep the normalization list to EXACTLY these 3 regex substitutions.
Rust equivalents using regex crate:
  re_ansi = Regex::new(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*m")?;
  re_timestamp = Regex::new(r"\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T[\d:.Z+-]+")?;
  re_pid = Regex::new(r"pid=\d+")?;

### Capture List Fix
The EXACT capture list from bash (27 files total):

CLI help (2 base + 15 subcommands = 17):
- am_help.txt (am --help)
- am_version.txt (am --version)
- am_serve-http_help.txt, am_serve-stdio_help.txt
- am_guard_help.txt, am_share_help.txt, am_doctor_help.txt
- am_config_help.txt, am_mail_help.txt
- am_agents_help.txt, am_tooling_help.txt, am_macros_help.txt
- am_contacts_help.txt, am_products_help.txt, am_archive_help.txt
- am_projects_help.txt, am_file_reservations_help.txt

MCP denials (5):
- mcp_deny_share.txt, mcp_deny_guard.txt, mcp_deny_doctor.txt
- mcp_deny_archive.txt, mcp_deny_migrate.txt

Stub encoder (5 files from 4 commands):
- stub_encode.txt (echo '{"id":1}' | stub --encode)
- stub_encode_stats_stdout.txt (echo '{"id":1}' | stub --encode --stats, stdout)
- stub_encode_stats_stderr.txt (same command, stderr)
- stub_help.txt (stub --help)
- stub_version.txt (stub --version)

Total: 17 + 5 + 5 = 27 files

### No list Mode in Bash
The `list` subcommand is a NEW feature (not in bash).
The bash only supports `capture` and `validate`.
Keep `list` as enhancement, but document it as new.

### No --filter, --json, --verbose Flags in Bash
These are all NEW features. Document as enhancements.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:03:46Z"},{"id":280,"issue_id":"br-mkn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS NOTE: T6.1/T6.3 Cross-Track Dependencies

The golden capture list includes help texts for new subcommands:
- am_ci_help.txt → requires Track 1 (T1.4 br-b9k2) to be complete
- am_flake-triage_help.txt → requires Track 2 (T2.5 br-32ax) to be complete
- am_bench_help.txt → requires Track 3 (T3.6 br-71g9) to be complete
- am_golden_help.txt → requires Track 6 itself (T6.3 br-2ygq) to be complete (self-referential)

HOWEVER: The golden capture system should be designed to be ADDITIVE.
New subcommands should automatically get their help text captured when
the capture list includes them. The capture list in T6.1 should be
data-driven (a registry of commands to capture), not hardcoded.

RECOMMENDATION: Do NOT add cross-track dependencies for golden captures.
Instead, design T6.1's capture registry to be extensible, and each track
adds its entry to the registry when wiring its CLI subcommand.

The self-referential golden capture (am_golden_help.txt) should simply
be added to the capture list as part of T6.3 wiring.

For the initial implementation, use the EXISTING 27 captures from bench_golden.sh.
New captures for ci/flake-triage/bench/check-inbox will be added when those
tracks complete — they don't block Track 6 from being implemented.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:04:58Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"br-mkn3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented native golden normalization/capture foundation:\n- Added crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/golden.rs with deterministic regex normalization rules (ANSI, ISO-8601 timestamp, pid=...) matching scripts/bench_golden.sh behavior.\n- Added capture helpers (capture_command, normalize_captured_output, capture_and_normalize_command) with unit tests.\n- Updated mode_matrix_harness golden snapshot tests to use normalization and to require UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 for fixture updates (no auto-overwrite during normal test runs).\n- Refreshed help snapshot fixtures via UPDATE_GOLDEN run and validated targeted tests.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:00:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-ms6k","title":"T9.8: Integrate native E2E runner into CI with phased equivalence validation","description":"## Objective
Integrate native E2E runner into CI/release workflows with phased fallback and equivalence validation.

## Work
- Add CI mode to run native suites and compare against shell outputs during transition.
- Define cutover gate: when native results become source of truth.
- Ensure gate report artifacts include native runner provenance.

## Deliverable
Controlled CI adoption of native E2E runner.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzurePine","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:25.996507924Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:14:38.317928064Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:14:38.317908928Z","close_reason":"Completed phased CI native-vs-shell equivalence integration with report provenance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","e2e","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ms6k","depends_on_id":"br-1dsl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:38.426862220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ms6k","depends_on_id":"br-1quw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:37.745822644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ms6k","depends_on_id":"br-1weca","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:38:52.755029901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ms6k","depends_on_id":"br-2xz9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:38.190735528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ms6k","depends_on_id":"br-nfen","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:37.963785915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":573,"issue_id":"br-ms6k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented `br-ms6k` (CI integration for native E2E with phased equivalence + provenance).

## Code changes

### 1) CI data model + phase contract (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs`)
- Added `CiE2ePhase` with env control `AM_CI_E2E_PHASE`:
  - `phase-a-shell-primary`
  - `phase-b-native-equivalence` (default)
  - `phase-c-native-primary`
- Added `E2eRunner` and per-gate `GateEquivalenceConfig`.
- Added report metadata structures:
  - `GateReportProvenance`
  - `GateEquivalenceRecord`
  - `GateEquivalenceSummary`
- Extended `GateReport` with optional fields:
  - `provenance`
  - `equivalence`
- Added `GateReport::with_metadata(...)` and preserved `GateReport::new(...)` compatibility path.

### 2) Phase-aware default gates + native cutover behavior (`ci.rs`)
- Added `default_gates_for_phase(phase)`.
- Converted E2E CI gates (dual_mode, mode_matrix, security_privacy, tui_a11y) to phase-aware command selection:
  - Phase A: shell primary + native shadow (non-blocking mismatch)
  - Phase B: native primary + shell shadow (mismatch enforced)
  - Phase C: native primary only
- Kept quick-mode skip behavior unchanged for these E2E gates.

### 3) Gate execution engine with equivalence checks (`ci.rs`)
- Refactored gate runner into:
  - `run_gate_once(...)`
  - `run_gate_with_metadata(...)`
  - public `run_gate(...)`
- Added enforced mismatch behavior for Phase B:
  - mismatch marks primary gate fail
  - diagnostic appended to stderr/error fields
- Aggregated shadow execution timing into gate elapsed time.
- Updated sequential and parallel runners to collect equivalence records and emit report metadata via `GateReport::with_metadata(...)`.

### 4) CLI wiring (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`)
- `handle_ci(...)` now:
  - resolves `CiE2ePhase::from_env()`
  - passes phase into `GateRunnerConfig`
  - uses `default_gates_for_phase(...)`
  - prints phase + source-of-truth in progress mode.

### 5) Docs
- `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`
  - switched CI authority to `am ci`
  - switched E2E gate commands to native `am e2e run ...`
  - updated artifact path expectations to `tests/artifacts/cli/...`
  - documented `AM_CI_E2E_PHASE` and expected provenance/equivalence fields.
- `docs/SPEC-script-migration-matrix.md`
  - `ci.sh` now mapped as migrated to `am ci`
  - `e2e_dual_mode.sh` / `e2e_mode_matrix.sh` updated to partial with native CI source-of-truth note.

## Tests added/updated
- Added CI unit tests covering:
  - phase parsing
  - phase-B native-primary + shell-shadow gate config
  - phase-C no-shadow behavior
  - enforced mismatch failure behavior
  - non-enforced mismatch behavior
  - report provenance/equivalence population.

## Validation evidence
- `export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/target-azurepine-am && cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --all-targets`
- `export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/target-azurepine-am && cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli ci::tests -- --nocapture`
- `export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/target-azurepine-am && cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test ci_integration -- --nocapture`
- `export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/target-azurepine-am && cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check -- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`

Note: `/tmp` is full on this host, so target dir isolation for validation was done under `/data/tmp`.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:14:38Z"}]}

Storage test count: 147 → 159.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:02:46.285673702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T07:02:52.095985996Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T07:02:52.095966640Z","close_reason":"12 new tests added, storage crate now at 159 tests (all passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-n6kw","title":"T9.10: Create next-wave bead plan for remaining heavy suite migrations","description":"## Objective
Create follow-on bead plan for remaining heavy E2E suite migrations (HTTP/share/archive/TUI interactions) after pilot cutover proves stable.

## Work
- Inventory remaining shell suites and complexity tiers.
- Propose batching strategy for subsequent migration waves.
- File child beads with priorities/dependencies so work remains executable.

## Deliverable
No migration stall after pilot phase; clear continuation roadmap in beads.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzurePine","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:26.782759419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:25.043269749Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:25.043250543Z","close_reason":"Completed: created and wired heavy-suite migration bead sequence (HTTP -> share/archive -> TUI)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","migration","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-n6kw","depends_on_id":"br-ms6k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:38.857039547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":575,"issue_id":"br-n6kw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed planning deliverable for next-wave heavy E2E migration after pilot cutover.

Inventory basis:
- am e2e list --json suite inventory
- scripts/e2e_*.sh complexity scan (HTTP/share/archive/TUI families are highest-risk remaining migration surfaces)
- native runner and CI phase review in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/e2e_runner.rs and crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs
- bv --robot-triage confirms Track 9 remains a high-impact blocker

Created follow-on beads and wired dependencies:
- br-dsdzo (T9.12): Port HTTP transport/parity suites to native E2E runner
- br-3ibsu (T9.13): Port share/archive heavy suites to native E2E runner
- br-3c7vp (T9.14): Port TUI interaction suites to native E2E runner with PTY adapter contracts

Dependency ordering:
- br-dsdzo depends on br-ms6k
- br-3ibsu depends on br-dsdzo
- br-3c7vp depends on br-3ibsu
- br-2avs now additionally depends on br-dsdzo, br-3ibsu, br-3c7vp so deprecation cannot close before wave completion

This removes migration planning stall and leaves an executable sequence for continuation.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-nfen","title":"T9.5: Port mode-matrix gate suite to native E2E runner","description":"## Objective
Port mode-matrix release-gate E2E suite to native runner.

## Work
- Recreate matrix combinations and expected outcomes.
- Preserve diagnostics for mismatched mode behavior.
- Ensure run duration remains acceptable for CI.

## Deliverable
Native mode-matrix suite with gate-grade confidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzurePine","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:25.013924386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:16.702687188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:00.260985438Z","close_reason":"Completed: native mode_matrix suite integrated and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mode-matrix","port"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-nfen","depends_on_id":"br-8zmc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:37.093815592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":554,"issue_id":"br-nfen","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed native mode_matrix migration: added native suite dispatch in e2e_runner, routed scripts/e2e_test.sh mode_matrix through native am e2e runner, updated mode_matrix_harness coverage for start/bench using clap-structural command discovery, refreshed help golden snapshot(s), and validated with mode_matrix_harness + am e2e run mode_matrix + scripts/e2e_test.sh mode_matrix.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-nhowr","title":"Track E: Coalesce Sharding","description":"**Background**

The `CoalesceMap` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/coalesce.rs` uses a single `Mutex<HashMap<K, Arc<Slot<V>>>>` (line 186) for all in-flight requests. Under high concurrency (10+ agents calling tools simultaneously), this becomes a contention point because every `execute_or_join` call must acquire the mutex to check or insert.

**Graveyard reference:** DashMap-style sharding. Split the single HashMap into N shards (N=16), each with its own Mutex. Route keys to shards via FNV hash. Contention drops by a factor of N because threads accessing different shards never contend.

**EV calculation:** (severity=5 * breadth=5 * feasibility=3) / (risk=3 * effort=2) = 12.5","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- 16-shard coalescer implemented with stable key-to-shard distribution
- Unit tests cover shard routing, leader/joiner correctness, timeout/error propagation, and cleanup invariants
- Integration tests prove behavioral isomorphism with single-mutex baseline across randomized schedules
- Stress/E2E concurrency tests show reduced contention and no stale inflight entries
- Bench artifacts include lock wait, throughput, latency tail, and fairness metrics
- Isomorphism proof includes deterministic replay harness and mismatch diagnostics
- tracing diagnostics capture shard id, wait duration, outcome class, and contention snapshots","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:23:40.716136869Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:23:40.716041380Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-nhowr","depends_on_id":"br-2fttz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:11.726492110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-nhowr","depends_on_id":"br-36tr5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:12.354301301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
